sections.paragraph:
βασκανία, ας, ἡ (s. βασκαίνω Pla., Demosth. et al.) envy (Dio Chrys. 28 [45], 5; Vi. Aesopi W 16 P.; 4 Macc 1:26; 2:15; TestIss 4:5; Philo; Jos., Ant. 3, 268) IRo 7:2.—Welles p. 321. M-M s.v. βασκαίνω.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Demosth Demosth , IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Dio Dio Chrys , I–II a.d.—List 5

Vi. Aesopi Vi. Aesopi , = Vitae Aesopi, I a.d.—List 5

TestIss TestIss = Testament of Issachar, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

Welles Welles = CW., Royal Correspondence in the Hellenistic Period—Lists 3, 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 171
